description.en: Play the Dreamweaver if you want to turn stress into strength and disrupt the minds of your enemies
When you choose a downtime move that clears Stress on yourself, you can also have an ally clear the same amount of Stress.

When you use your **Dream Eater** feature, place a number of tokens equal to your Spellcast trait on this card. When you make a Spellcast Roll or reaction roll, you can spend any number of these tokens to roll an equal number of d4s and add the total to the roll. When you start a rest, clear any unspent tokens on this card and gain a Hope per token cleared.
Whenever an adversary within Far range marks a Stress, you can **mark a Stress** to make them temporarily *Vulnerable* and remove a Fear from the GM's Fear pool.

Once per session, when an adversary within Far range succeeds on an attack roll, you can **spend 3 Hope** to make a reaction roll using your Spellcast trait against their Difficulty. On a success, they become temporarily *Stunned* and the attack is negated. While *Stunned*, they can't use reactions and can't take any other actions until they clear this condition.
Play the Spiritcaller if you want to bind spirits into physical totems and wield their blessings

You carry three handcrafted totems, each housing a spirit that empowers you. Describe each totem's appearance and the nature of the spirit it contains. You can invoke each totem once per long rest:

- **Protective Totem** When you mark an Armor Slot, reduce the severity of the attack by two thresholds instead of one.

- **Muddling Totem** Make a target within Close range temporarily Muddled. While Muddled, they have disadvantage on attack rolls.

- **Leeching Totem** Choose a target within Melee range. They mark a Hit Point, and you clear a Hit Point.

When you invoke one of the totems from your **Totemic Binding** feature, you can also choose one of the following:

- You gain a Hope.

- You clear an Armor Slot.

Additionally, you can **mark a Stress** to grant the same benefit to an ally within Close range.
Your connections to the spirits within your totems have grown stronger. You can invoke each of your **Totemic Binding** feature's totems twice instead of once per long rest.
